description:
## The change

After NA.1+NA.2, the atoms named "nil"/"true"/"false" with reserved
IDs 0/1/2 are technically just atoms — naive print would render
them as ":nil"/":true"/":false". But the source-level convention
(and existing `print(nil)` output, pinned by fixtures) is to render
them bareword. Re-establish that as an explicit rendering rule for
the three reserved IDs.

### Update

  runtime/src/fz_value.rs:
    `debug::render_atom`:
      Before formatting `:atom_N` / `:name`, check if the atom ID
      equals one of the three reserved IDs. If so, render bareword:
        atom_id == NIL_ATOM_ID   → "nil"
        atom_id == TRUE_ATOM_ID  → "true"
        atom_id == FALSE_ATOM_ID → "false"
      Other atoms render `:name` as today.

  src/types.rs:
    Descr Display impl:
      atom_lit("nil") renders as `nil` (not `:nil`).
      atom_lit("true") renders as `true`.
      atom_lit("false") renders as `false`.
      Other atom literals render `:name` as today.
      bool_t() may render as `true | false` or `bool` — pick the
      shorter convention; document.

### Why these three are special-cased

Document the rationale in a doc comment near the rendering site:
the three are reserved at compile time because the language has
keywords for them (`nil`, `true`, `false`) and source/print
symmetry is the design intent. Other atoms have no keyword; the
leading colon distinguishes the atom literal from a Var or call.

### Goldens

Spec / CLIF goldens may shift if any Display impl rendered atom
literals differently. expected.txt should NOT change — the
runtime's render path was already producing "nil"/"true"/"false"
for those values pre-epic; this re-establishes that after NA.1
changed the underlying representation.

## Removes

Nothing structural. Adds three branches in the rendering match.

## Depends on

NA.2.

## Hazards

  - If a user writes `:nil` (atom literal with leading colon)
    expecting it to be a different value from `nil` (the keyword),
    after this epic they're the same value and print the same.
    Audit fixtures: none use `:nil` / `:true` / `:false` (per
    pre-flight grep). Safe.
  - Any test asserting specific render strings for atom IDs 0/1/2
    needs updating.
title: fz-NA.3: pretty-print — reserved atoms render without colon
